name: React
analogy: Like LEGO blocks for websites — you build small pieces and snap them together
whatItDoes: React is a JavaScript library made by Meta for building user interfaces. You create reusable components — like a button, a form, or a navigation bar — and compose them together to build full pages. When the data changes, React automatically updates only the parts of the page that need to change, without reloading the whole thing.
whyYouUseIt: Every visual element in SafetyIQ — the incident form, the stats dashboard, the navigation menu — is a React component. Because components are reusable, I built the incident card once and it appears consistently everywhere it is needed. This keeps the UI consistent across the HSE reporting screens.
whenToSwap: If you want something that feels simpler and compiles to smaller bundles, Svelte is worth exploring. Vue.js has a gentler learning curve and is popular in Asia-Pacific teams. Angular is better suited to large enterprise teams that want strict structure enforced by the framework.
